revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Designierte Konservierungstechniken]][[en:Conservation Techniques for Cultural Properties]][[ja:選定保存技術]]
In 1975 the Japanese government added a new chapter to the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (1950) to provide for "traditional techniques or craftsmanship that are indispensable to the preservation of cultural property and for which preservation measures shall be taken". Preservation techniques are selected in relation to both [[:en:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|tangible]] and [[:en:Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan|intangible]] cultural properties and a holder or preservation body is recognized for each technique.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.unesco.org/culture/natlaws/media/pdf/japan/japan_law_protectionproperty_entno.pdf |title=Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (1950, last amendment 2007) |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |accessdate=2 May 2011|qq=}}</ref><ref name="Bunkachō">{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/english/pdf/h21_chapter_06.pdf |title=Preservation and Utilization of Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |page=41 |accessdate=2 May 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110523235255/http://www.bunka.go.jp/english/pdf/h21_chapter_06.pdf |archive-date=23 May 2011 |url-status=dead |qq=}}</ref><ref name="Larsen">{{cite book |title=Architectural Preservation in Japan |author=Larsen, Knut Einar |publisher=[[ICOMOS]] International Wood Committee |year=1994 |pages=40f |ISBN=82-519-1432-9|qq=}}</ref> In support of the {{nihongo|Selected Conservation Techniques|選定保存技術|qq=}}, the government provides funding for and conducts programmes relating to documentation, training, development, promotion and public education. Recognition is thus made and measures taken to combat some of the most serious conservation issues, namely the lack of specialist craftsmen, the loss of knowledge of traditional techniques, and the availability of suitable tools and materials. These issues are of particular relevance in Japan due to the sophistication and inherent susceptibility of much of its art and architecture.<ref name="Bunkachō"/><ref name="Larsen"/> The list below is compiled from the database of cultural properties maintained by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]], with translations, glosses and listed holders (as of 2004) following those published by the Asia/Pacific Cultural Centre for {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} (ACCU).<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp |title=Database of Cultural Properties |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=16 February 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120222235539/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp |archive-date=22 February 2012 |url-status=dead |qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.accu.or.jp/ich/en/pdf/2004_Tokyo.pdf |title=2004 Workshop on Inventory-making for Intangible Cultural Heritage Management |publisher=Asia/Pacific Centre for {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} (ACCU) |pages=200–203 |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> ==Selected Conservation Techniques== ===Tangible Cultural Properties=== 43 techniques {| class="wikitable sortable" width="100%" style="background:#ffffff;" |- !align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" | Name !align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" class="unsortable" | Remarks !style="background:#ffdead;" | Holder !align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" | Year !align="left" style="background:#ffdead;" class="unsortable"|Picture |- | {{nihongo|Production of roofing tiles|屋根瓦製作 (鬼師)|yane kawara seisaku (onishi)|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/9 |title=屋根瓦製作 (鬼師) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/o/onigawara.htm |title=Onigawara |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || {{ill|Onigawara|en|Onigawara|qid=Q1895128|lt=Onigawara|1=Onigawara}} || Individual (Akio Kobayashi, b. 1921) || 1988 || [[File:Onigawara.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Kiku-jutsu techniques for marking when constructing wooden building (modern)|規矩術 (近世規矩)|kiku-jutsu (kinsei-kiku)|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/12 |title=規矩術 (近世規矩) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kiku.htm |title=Kiku |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || lit. "measuring with a carpenter's square" || Individual (Takeo Mochida, b. 1931) || 1993 || |- | {{nihongo|Kiku-jutsu techniques for marking when constructing wooden building (ancient)|規矩術 (古式規矩)|kiku-jutsu (koshiki-kiku)|qq=}}<ref name="Larsen"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/10 |title=規矩術 (古式規矩) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || In particular for projecting eaves; developed since the twelfth century || Individual (Takeo Mochida, b. 1931) || 1991 || |- | {{nihongo|Smithery of metal fittings|金具鍛冶|kanagu kaji|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000074 |title=金具鍛冶 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kanagu.htm |title=Kanagu |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Mayoshio Yokoya, b. 1931) || 2002 || [[File:Japanese metal fittings Rokuyou.svg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Manufacture of [[kinkarakami]]|金唐紙製作|kinkarakami seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000091 |title=金唐紙製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kinkara/english.html |title=Kinkarakami |publisher=Takashi Ueda (Bearer of Technique) |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kinkarakawa.htm |title=Kinkarakawa |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || Gold-embossed wallpaper, used in western-style buildings of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} to {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} periods; cf. kinkarakawa or 'leather wallpaper' || Individual || 2005 || |- | {{nihongo|Making of traditional fittings such as [[:en:Shōji|sliding doors]]|建具製作|tategu seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000071 |title=建具製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref name="Tategu">{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/t/tategu.htm |title=Tategu |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || Includes [[:en:Shōji|Shōji]], {{ill|Fusuma|en|Fusuma|qid=Q1069573|lt=Fusuma|1=Fusuma}}, || Individual (Tadashi Suzuki, b. 1936) || 1999 || [[File:Farsari house interior.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Production of [[:en:architectural models|model buildings]]|建造物模型製作|kenzōbutsu mokei seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/14 |title=建造物模型製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Yushuhiro Wada, b. 1932) || 1999 || [[File:Kairyuoji2.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Restoration of {{ill|Japanese armour|en|Japanese armour|qid=Q3671179|lt=armour|1=Japanese armour}}|甲冑修理|katchū shūri|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/49 |title=甲冑修理 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Masami Ozawa, b. 1953) || 1998 || [[File:Samurai with sword.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Plastering (traditional {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyokabe|1=Kyoto}} walls)|左官 (古式京壁)|sakan (koshiki kyō-kabe)|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000073 |title=左官 (古式京壁) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Haruo Sato, b. 1924) || 2001 || [[File:Wall of Kyoto Imperial Palace.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Plastering|左官 (漆喰塗)|sakan (shikkuinuri)|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000067 |title=左官 (漆喰塗) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.omotesenke.jp/english/chanoyu/4_2_4.html |title=Clay walls |publisher=[[Omotesenke]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || Glue, brine, clay, and thread are mixed into the {{ill|Lime (material)|en|Lime (material)|qid=Q250423|lt=lime|1=Lime (material)}} || Individual (Isokichi Okui, b. 1919) || 1998 || |- | {{nihongo|Restoration of {{ill|Lacquerware|en|Lacquerware|qid=Q368972|lt=lacquer wares|1=Lacquerware}}|漆工品修理|shikkōhin shūri|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/1 |title=漆工品修理 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref name="Urushi">{{cite book |title=Urushi: Proceedings of the Urushi Study Group, June 10-27, 1985, Tokyo |editor=Bromelle, N. S. |editor2=Smith, Perry |chapter=The Preservation and Handing Down of Traditional Urushi Art Techniques in Japan |author=Yagihashi, Shin |publisher=[[Getty Conservation Institute]] |year=1988 |ISBN=0-89236-096-8 |pages=85f|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Kenichi Kitamura, b. 1938) || 1994 || [[File:Makie-enlargement.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Crafting and restoration of metal fittings of Jōdai ornaments|上代飾金具製作修理|jōdai kazari kanagu seisaku shūri|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/4 |title=上代飾金具製作修理 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || Includes [[mikoshi]], [[Zushi (shrines)|zushi]] || Individual (Sōtaro Kanae, b. 1908) || 1977 || [[File:O-mikoshi 197152337 a863c9050e o.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Manufacture of [[tatami]]|畳製作|tatami seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000085 |title=畳製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual || 1929 || [[File:Men Making Tatami Mats, 1860 - ca. 1900.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Stone tile roofing|石盤葺|sekibanbuki|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000092 |title=石盤葺 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual || 2005 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of {{ill|Nail (fastener)|en|Nail (fastener)|qid=Q37077|lt=nails|1=Nail (fastener)}} made of {{ill|Bamboo|en|Bamboo|qid=Q670887|lt=Bamboo|1=Bamboo}}|竹釘製作|takekugi seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000066 |title=竹釘製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/t/takekugi.htm |title=Takekugi |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Yoshiharu Ishizuka, b. 1922) || 1998 || |- | {{nihongo|{{ill|Casting|en|Casting|qid=Q496098|lt=Casting|1=Casting}} production|鋳物製作|imono seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000072 |title=鋳物製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Shūichi Otani, b. 1933) || 1999 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of [[karakami]] paper|唐紙製作|karakami seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/8 |title=唐紙製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karakami.htm |title=Karakami |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Kenkichi Senda, b. 1942) || 1999 || |- | {{nihongo|Manufacture of [[kiribako|boxes]] made of {{ill|Paulownia|en|Paulownia|qid=Q165173|lt=Paulownia|1=Paulownia}} wood for containing art and craft objects|美術工芸品保存桐箱製作|bijutsu kōgeihin hozon kiribako seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/7 |title=美術工芸品保存桐箱製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Yūichi Maeda, b. 1927) || 1980 || |- | {{nihongo|Manufacture of ancient textiles (such as gold {{ill|Brocade|en|Brocade|qid=Q649022|lt=Brocade|1=Brocade}}) for mounting|表具用古代裂 (金襴等) 製作|hyōgu-yō kodai-gire (kinran tō) seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/3 |title=表具用古代裂 (金襴等) 製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/hyougu.htm |title=Hyougu |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kinran.htm |title=Kinran |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Japanese Scroll Paintings: A Handbook of Mounting Techniques |author=Koyano, Masako |publisher=[[American Institute for Conservation]] |year=1979 |ISBN=978-0-933098-01-5|qq=}}</ref> || For {{ill|Kakemono|en|Kakemono|qid=Q277583|lt=Kakemono|1=Kakemono}} etc. || Individual || 2007 || |- | {{nihongo|Manufacture of brushes for mounting|表具用刷毛製作|hyōguyō hage seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000084 |title=表具用刷毛製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/hake.htm |title=Hake |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual || 1958 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of Udagami handmade paper for mounting on sliding doors|表具用手漉和紙 (宇陀紙) 製作|hyōguyō tesuki washi (udagami) seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/5 |title=表具用手漉和紙 (宇陀紙) 製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || {{ill|Washi|en|Washi|qid=Q846299|lt=Washi|1=Washi}} || Individual (Hiroyuki Hukunishi, b. 1930) || 1978 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of Misugami handmade paper for mounting|表具用手漉和紙 (美栖紙) 製作|hyōguyō tesuki washi (misugami) seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/2 |title=表具用手漉和紙 (美栖紙) 製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || {{ill|Washi|en|Washi|qid=Q846299|lt=Washi|1=Washi}} || Individual || 2009 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of Hoshū handmade paper for mounting|表具用手漉和紙 (補修紙) 製作|hyōguyō tesuki washi (hoshūshi) seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/15 |title=表具用手漉和紙 (補修紙) 製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || {{ill|Washi|en|Washi|qid=Q846299|lt=Washi|1=Washi}} || Individual || 2007 || |- | {{nihongo|Production of brushes for mounting|表具用打刷毛製作|hyōguyō uchibake seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/57 |title=表具用打刷毛製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Genjirō Hujii, b. 1920) || 1998 || |- | {{nihongo|{{ill|Joiner|en|Joiner|qid=Q326358|lt=Joiner|1=Joiner}}y for mounting|表装建具製作|hyōsō tategu seisaku|qq=}}<ref name="Tategu"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/16 |title=表装建具製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/hyousou.htm |title=Hyousou |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual || 1995 || [[File:Wind God and Thunder God Screens by Tawaraya Sotatsu hi-res.png|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Dyeing with true {{ill|Indigo dye|en|Indigo dye|qid=Q422662|lt=indigo|1=Indigo dye}}|本藍染|hon aizome|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/47 |title=本藍染 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Yoshio Mori, b. 1941) || 1996 || [[File:Indian indigo dye lump.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Woodwork restoration|木工品修理|mokkohin shūri|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/56 |title=木工品修理 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Hiroshi Sakurai, b. 1950) || 1997 || |- | {{nihongo|Gathering [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]] bark|檜皮採取|hiwada saishiki|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000070 |title=檜皮採取 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual (Yutaka Ono, b. 1934) || 1999 || [[File:Kyoto Toji Hiwadabuki C0990.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Crafting of decorative metal fittings|錺金具製作|kazari kanagu seisaku|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/00000068 |title=錺金具製作 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kazarikanagu.htm |title=Kazarikanagu |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Individual || 1998 (Yasunosuke Morimoto, b. 1928) || [[File:Nikko Toshogu Yomeimon M3247.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Roofing with roofing tiles|屋根瓦葺 (本瓦葺)|yanegawarabuki (hongawarabuki)|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/13 |title=屋根瓦葺 (本瓦葺) |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/hongawarabuki.htm |title=Hongawarabuki |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Joint (Kiyokazu Yamamoto, b. 1932; Mitsuo Teramoto, b. 1946; group also) || 1994 || [[File:Byodoin-Phoenix-M1234.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Roofing with {{ill|Thatching|en|Thatching|qid=Q874531|lt=thatch|1=Thatching}}|茅葺|kayabuki|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/36 |title=茅葺 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kayabuki.htm |title=Kayabuki |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || || Joint (Ryōzō Sumida, b. 1926; National Organization for Conservation of Roofing Techniques for Temples and Shrines) || 1980 || [[File:Shikokumura07s3200.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Woodwork for building|建造物木工|kenzōbutsu mokkō|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/35 |title=建造物木工 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || Joint (Shoji Matsuura, b. 1929; Association of Conservation Techniques for Cultural Property Buildings) || 1976 || [[File:Sumitsubo.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Roof-thatching using [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]] bark or boards |檜皮葺・柿葺|hiwada-buki/kokera-buki|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/6 |title=檜皮葺・柿葺 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/h/hiwadabuki.htm |title=Hiwadabuki |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kokerabuki.htm |title=Kokerabuki |publisher=[[Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System]] |accessdate=4 May 2011|qq=}}</ref> || cf. [[:en:Shake (shingle)|shake]] roofing || Joint (Yasuo Onishi, b. 1925; National Organization for Conservation of Roofing Techniques for Temples and Shrines) || 1976 || [[File:Hogonji00bs3872.jpg|100px]] |- | {{nihongo|Buildings colouration|建造物彩色|kenzōbutsu saishiki|qq=}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/304/6 |title=建造物彩色 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=16 February 2012|qq=}}</ref> || || part_of (Organization for Conserving Cultural Properties Related to {{ill|Shrines and Temples of Nikkō|en|Shrines and Temples of Nikkō|qid=Q1063133|lt=Shrines and Temples of Nikkō|1=Shrines and Temples of Nikkō}} || 1979 || [[File:20100727 Nikko Tosho-gu Yomeimon 5934.jpg|100px]] |- |
